title: AAR: Game Clock, Timeout, Overtime, Referee, Injury & Efficiency Overhaul
scope: Major overhaul touching six systems: game clock model, timeout logic, postseason overtime, referee bias, injury catalog, and KenPom-style efficiency metrics. The common thread: making the simulation behave like a real broadcast game, not a spreadsheet.

rationale.item: A TD at 1:01 left the opposing team starting at 0:17 (44 seconds vanished) — game clock and play clock conflated
Victory formation triggering with 4+ minutes remaining
Timeouts hoarded until final 2-5 minutes; games had only 1-2 charged timeouts with both teams ending with 3-4 unused per half
Playoff games could end in a tie (UTSA 45 — Grambling 45)
Trailing teams could punt on 6th down

actions.description: Game clock / play clock split: game clock burns only action time (3-9s) after scores/penalties/incompletes; clock state persists across drives
Victory formation restricted to time_remaining<=50 seconds in Q4
New timeout categories (8 types) with coaching intelligence, 3-min warning free timeout treatment, trait modifiers
Postseason overtime: successive 8-minute OT quarters until someone wins (is_playoff=True, week>=900)
Punt logic fix: trailing team never punts on 6th down; blocks punts with <=30 seconds in Q4
Referee bias system: 300 named refs, 3-person crews, blown call types, coach challenge system (3/game)
Injury catalog expanded 83→190 entries with OOTP-style metadata (freq/reinjury/nagging/surgery/inf_run/inf_kick/inf_lateral)
Injury Report UI with filters, metric cards, histogram, nagging/re-injury watch
KenPom-style efficiency metrics: 25 new TeamRecord accumulators; glossary at docs/kenpom_glossary.md
Box score improvements: team names, individual stat leaders, time of possession, organized sections, declined penalties

validation_claims.claim: Clock verification: TD at 6:21 → next play at 6:14 (7s elapsed — action time only); play after at 6:08 (6s elapsed — clock still stopped); play after at 5:38 (30s elapsed — normal play clock resumes)
validation_claims.check_method: Run a game and verify post-score clock state in play-by-play

validation_claims.claim: 20-game timeout sample: avg TOs per game 6.3 (was ~2); TOs remaining avg/team/half ~2 (was 3-4); category breakdown: scheme reset 34%, momentum 26%, offensive clock 24%, strategic 12%, kneel stop 4%
validation_claims.check_method: Run 20-game batch sim and count timeout categories

validation_claims.claim: 200-game playoff sample: 4.5% of playoff games go to OT; 0% ties in playoff mode; regular season 3.5% ties (unchanged)
validation_claims.check_method: Run 200-game playoff simulation and count OT occurrences and ties

non_actions.item: Opponent-adjusted efficiency (AdjO/AdjD weighted by schedule strength)
Nagging injury flare-ups (elevated re-injury risk for 2-3 weeks after return)
Conference-assigned referee crews (refs can't officiate own conference)
KenPom UI display on stats page
non_actions.reason: Data is in the API, needs frontend

non_actions.item: Icing the kicker / fresh legs categories need more testing with varied rosters

open_hooks: KenPom glossary at docs/kenpom_glossary.md documents all 10 metrics with basketball analytics equivalents
section_headers_original: AAR: Game Clock, Timeout, Overtime, Referee, Injury & Efficiency Overhaul
unmapped_content: Branch: claude/referee-bias-system-B6KAy. KenPom metric table with 10 metrics: Raw O (pts/10 drives = AdjO), Raw D (opp pts/10 drives = AdjD), EK% (value-weighted kick success = eFG%), TO%, TOD%, LR% (lateral recovery = OR%), FDR (penalty first downs = FTR), RLE (rush yards/carry = 2P%), KP% (kick pass completion = 3P%), Adj T (plays/game = AdjT). Injury profile shifted toward soccer/basketball (soft tissue, concussions elevated for no-hard-helmet sport). Overtime: fresh 4 TOs per OT team; 3-min warning applies; energy +20 recovery; alternating first possession; max 5 OT periods; OT plays as Q5/Q6 etc. Timeout coaching intelligence: 3-min warning = free timeout; good clock managers save 1 TO; Q4 crunch time suppresses non-clock categories; timeout_hoarder trait resists 30-60%; timeout_sprinter burns 25-50% faster. Bug fixes in timeout section: _drive_yards was always 0 via getattr fallback; _drive_consecutive_negative tracked; _consecutive_opponent_scores tracked.
parser_notes: Date confirmed: 2026-03-25. Very large scope — 6 distinct systems in one AAR. 'What's Not Done Yet' maps to non_actions.
